| `rankfold-neuralfold-and-artifact-compression.core` | `rankfold-neuralfold-and-artifact-compression` | RankFold/NeuralFold-style artifact compression belongs as a bounded implementation hypothesis with probe-route fallback and residual coding. | Design rationale | argument | `rankfold_neuralfold`, `rankfold_compressor`, `bbvca_v9`, `cgs`, `bugbrain` | Source notes available for all 5 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 5 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `semantic-representation-and-tree-structured-models.core` | `semantic-representation-and-tree-structured-models` | Tree-structured semantic representations should be treated as optional representation substrates that must prove grounding, utility, and interoperability. | Design rationale | argument | `treellm`, `spinoza`, `verification_bandwidth`, `cognitive_compilation`, `cgs`, `circle_ai_architectures`, `coilra_multicoil_rope` | Source notes available for all 7 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 7 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `resource-economics-and-token-budgets.core` | `resource-economics-and-token-budgets` | The stack should account for token budgets, verification tax, load stability, and risk-adjusted inference value. | Design rationale | argument | `tokenmana`, `planforge`, `coherence_exchange`, `simulation_scaling`, `viea`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `coilra_multicoil_rope` | Source notes available for all 7 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 7 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints.core` | `simulation-fidelity-and-physical-constraints` | Simulation and fidelity claims should be bounded by resource constraints and used as design constraints rather than assumed unlimited substrates. | Design rationale | argument | `simulation_scaling`, `cgs`, `rankfold_neuralfold`, `tokenmana`, `alignment_field` | Source notes available for all 5 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 5 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `mathematical-and-search-substrates.core` | `mathematical-and-search-substrates` | Coils, calculi, and geometric search belong in the stack as optional specialist substrates until tests show where they improve search, routing, or compression. | Design rationale | argument | `genesiscode`, `temporal_coil_research`, `cognitive_compilation`, `treellm`, `simulation_scaling`, `coilmoecot`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_architectures`, `proof_carrying_circular_computation`, `theseus_circle_transfer` | Source notes available for all 10 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 10 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `circle-calculus-and-proof-carrying-ai-contracts.core` | `circle-calculus-and-proof-carrying-ai-contracts` | Proof-carrying AI contracts should package finite structural facts as theorem-linked receipts with deterministic fields, consumer gates, validation commands, and explicit non-claims. | Design rationale | argument | `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `rope_position_certifier`, `proof_carrying_circular_computation`, `circle_ai_architectures` | Source notes available for all 5 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 5 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `coil-attention-cyclic-memory-and-recurrence-contracts.core` | `coil-attention-cyclic-memory-and-recurrence-contracts` | Coil attention and memory contracts should be used as structural guardrails for cyclic memory, sparse coverage, recurrence schedules, and work-budget admission, not as claims of retrieval or reasoning quality. | Design rationale | argument | `coil_attention_memory`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `theseus_circle_transfer`, `vcm_public` | Source notes available for all 4 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 4 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `coilra-multicoil-rope-and-cyclic-mixers.core` | `coilra-multicoil-rope-and-cyclic-mixers` | CoilRA, MultiCoil RoPE, and cyclic mixers should be treated as optional specialist substrates whose structural guarantees must be paired with dense, LoRA, RoPE, learned, recurrent, or state-space baselines before adoption. | Design rationale | argument | `coilra_multicoil_rope`, `rope_position_certifier`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `theseus_circle_transfer`, `circle_ai_architectures` | Source notes available for all 5 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 5 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `executable-specifications-and-lean-proof-envelope.core` | `executable-specifications-and-lean-proof-envelope` | Lean proofs and executable specs should target small invariants: authority, support states, SCF replacement, job lifecycle, context adequacy, and residual records. | Design rationale | argument | `genesiscode`, `spinoza`, `scf`, `talos`, `viea`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `rope_position_certifier`, `proof_carrying_circular_computation` | Source notes available for all 9 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 9 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `benchmark-ratchets-and-anti-goodhart-evidence.core` | `benchmark-ratchets-and-anti-goodhart-evidence` | Benchmark ratchets should preserve regressions, create harder frontiers, record residuals, and resist Goodhart pressure. | Design rationale | argument | `benchmaxxing`, `rmi`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `uat`, `coherence_exchange`, `tokenmana`, `moecot`, `road_to_agi`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `theseus_architecture_gate`, `theseus_self_evolution_system`, `theseus_circle_transfer` | Source notes available for all 12 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 12 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `policy-optimization-and-learning-from-feedback.core` | `policy-optimization-and-learning-from-feedback` | Policy optimization is the stack's learning actuator: it can update planners, routers, context selectors, verifiers, execution policies, and generators, but governance decides which feedback is admissible and whether any update may be promoted. | Design rationale | argument | `benchmaxxing`, `rmi`, `spinoza`, `verification_bandwidth`, `talos`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `octopus_router`, `scf`, `tokenmana`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot` | Source notes available for all 12 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 12 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `integrated-reference-architecture.core` | `integrated-reference-architecture` | The stack can be specified as an integrated reference architecture with typed handoffs, authority stops, artifacts, evidence updates, and self-improvement gates. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `talos`, `spinoza`, `octopus_router`, `rmi`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `cgs`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`, `vcm_editable`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `theseus_plan_compiler`, `theseus_self_evolution_system`, `theseus_architecture_gate`, `theseus_operator_os`, `theseus_circle_transfer` | Source notes available for all 20 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 20 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `project-theseus-as-report-first-implementation-reference.core` | `project-theseus-as-report-first-implementation-reference` | Project Theseus should be mined as a report-first implementation reference for RMI, showing how pressure, residuals, gates, ledgers, operator work boards, checkpoints, and self-evolution governance can instantiate the ASI stack without promoting local results as general capability claims. | Design rationale | argument | `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `theseus_plan_compiler`, `theseus_self_evolution_system`, `theseus_architecture_gate`, `theseus_operator_os`, `theseus_circle_transfer` | Source notes available for all 6 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 6 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `prototype-roadmap.core` | `prototype-roadmap` | The prototype should grow in staged increments from source matrix and artifact graph to governed capability replacement. | Design rationale | argument | `viea`, `benchmaxxing`, `scf`, `vcm_public`, `planforge`, `beastbrain`, `beastbrain_timeless`, `bugbrain`, `moecot`, `moecot_md`, `road_to_agi`, `coherence_exchange`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `theseus_plan_compiler`, `theseus_self_evolution_system`, `theseus_architecture_gate`, `theseus_operator_os`, `theseus_circle_transfer`, `circle_ai_contract_suite` | Source notes available for all 19 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 19 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `living-book-methodology.core` | `living-book-methodology` | The living book should model the ASI stack discipline through manifest-driven Quarto, source queues, claim/evidence matrices, proof manifests, tests, changelogs, and releases. | Design rationale | argument | `benchmaxxing`, `viea`, `bugbrain`, `cognitive_loop_closure`, `moecot`, `moecot_md`, `road_to_agi` | Source notes available for all 7 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 7 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |
| `open-research-agenda-and-bibliography-plan.core` | `open-research-agenda-and-bibliography-plan` | The bibliography and research agenda should map source families, external literature queues, missing papers, proof targets, experiment backlogs, and chapter insertion rules. | Design rationale | argument | `verification_bandwidth`, `benchmaxxing`, `alignment_field`, `scf`, `planforge`, `vcm_public`, `spinoza`, `talos`, `rmi`, `cgs`, `genesiscode`, `simulation_scaling`, `moecot`, `coilmoecot`, `road_to_agi`, `vcm_editable`, `field_of_god_ai_constitution`, `project_theseus_whitepaper`, `circle_calculus_core`, `circle_ai_contract_suite`, `theseus_circle_transfer` | Source notes available for all 21 assigned sources; support remains at the recorded state until an accepted evidence transition, proof, test, or source-derived promotion justifies movement. | All 21 assigned source notes explicitly list this chapter; mapping remains chapter-level, not claim-level support. | No exact claim-level source-note mapping recorded yet. | Map the exact claim text to specific source-note mechanisms/evidence and define or run tests before raising support state. |

## Claim Labels

| Label | Meaning |
|---|---|
| Demonstrated | Shown by a recorded artifact, derivation, implementation, or source-backed example in scope. |
| Measured | Quantified by a recorded measurement or benchmark result. |
| Mechanized | Expressed as runnable code, an executable schema, or a formal proof target. |
| Hypothesized | Proposed as a testable claim that still needs evidence. |
| Design rationale | An architectural choice supported by reasoning and constraints. |
| Speculative | Exploratory or conjectural; do not use as a deployed system guarantee. |

## Support States

| State | Meaning |
|---|---|
| unsupported | Not yet supported; likely remove or mark as speculative. |
| argument | Supported by reasoning only. |
| source-derived | Derived from supplied source papers. |
| prototype-backed | Implemented in a prototype but not robustly tested. |
| synthetic-test-backed | Supported by controlled synthetic tests. |
| empirical-test-backed | Supported by external or realistic tests. |
| external-literature-backed | Supported by third-party literature. |
| deprecated | Superseded, merged, or retired; retained for lineage and review. |
| refuted | Contradicted by later evidence or tests; retained to preserve negative results. |
